description: Foundational high school chemistry covering lab safety, atomic theory, the periodic table, chemical bonding, nomenclature, reaction types, stoichiometry, solutions, acids and bases, and gas laws. Builds essential skills for senior chemistry courses.

topics.name: Unit 1: Foundations of Matter & Safety
topics.description: Laboratory safety protocols, classification of matter, physical and chemical properties, separation techniques, and foundational measurement skills.

topics.name: Unit 2: Atomic Structure & Theory
topics.description: Historical atomic models, subatomic particles, isotopes, and early quantum theory leading to the modern understanding of the atom.

topics.name: Unit 3: The Periodic Table & Trends
topics.description: Organization of the periodic table, periodic law, atomic radius, ionization energy, electronegativity, and other elemental trends.

topics.name: Unit 4: Chemical Bonding
topics.description: Ionic, covalent, and metallic bonding, Lewis structures, bond polarity, and an introduction to molecular geometry.

topics.name: Unit 5: Chemical Nomenclature
topics.description: Systematic naming of ionic compounds, covalent compounds, acids, and simple organic compounds using IUPAC conventions.

topics.name: Unit 6: Types of Chemical Reactions
topics.description: Synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, double replacement, and combustion reactions, including predicting products and balancing equations.

topics.name: Unit 7: The Mole & Molar Mass
topics.description: Avogadro's number, mole concept, conversions between mass, moles, and number of particles, and an introduction to empirical formulas.

topics.name: Unit 8: Stoichiometry I (Basic)
topics.description: Mole ratios from balanced equations, mass-to-mass stoichiometric calculations, and introductory limiting reactant problems.

topics.name: Unit 9: Stoichiometry II (Advanced)
topics.description: Limiting and excess reactants, percent yield, theoretical yield, and multi-step stoichiometric calculations involving solutions and gases.

topics.name: Unit 10: Solutions & Solubility
topics.description: Concentration calculations (molarity, percent concentration), solution preparation, factors affecting solubility, and precipitation reactions.

topics.name: Unit 11: Acids, Bases, & pH
topics.description: Properties of acids and bases, Arrhenius and Brønsted-Lowry theories, pH scale, strong vs. weak acids/bases, and neutralization reactions.

topics.name: Unit 12: Gases & Atmospheric Chemistry
topics.description: Properties of gases, kinetic molecular theory, gas laws (Boyle's, Charles's, Gay-Lussac's, combined), ideal gas law, and gas stoichiometry.
